mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when rates are in the accommodation zone.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces forward earnings growth expectations. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows, or joint decline events produce positive returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average signals expanding supply-side capacity and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice earnings upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO > MA are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market that increases wage pressures and inflation expectations. This interaction triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as markets reprice inflation risk and expected real returns.
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows, or joint signal produces negative returns in < 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth above 0.3%, the Fed is in restrictive tightening mode. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as higher real discount rates compress equity valuations and growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows, or 60-day returns in this regime are positive in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports equity earnings growth. This always-on mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days when the economy is in expansion mode.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of GDPC1 growth > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and forward housing demand, which cascades into reduced construction employment and consumer wealth effects. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
